name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: Attorney General William Barr must resign. Now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f792/6cd27893/53e0b92c/15866983/1288247546/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | For our democracy to endure, it must have an _independent_ and _impartial_ judicial system--one that puts the rule of law ahead of personal and political considerations. **That's why I am calling on Attorney General William Barr to resign _immediately_. ** **[Please add your voice to the more than 40,361 Americans that have already called on him to resign.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f792/6cd27893/53e0b92c/15866980/1288247546/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/barr-resign/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2020-02-barr-resign-email")** [Demand that Barr resign](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f792/6cd27893/53e0b92c/15866980/1288247546/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/barr-resign/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2020-02-barr-resign-email") Barr claims to be independent from Donald Trump. But, as we saw with his recent interference in the Roger Stone case, the truth is _very_ different. _The facts?_
* Roger Stone is guilty, and the career prosecutors on his case recommended a specific sentence for the crimes committed.
* Then, Trump whined publicly (via Twitter, of course) that his friend was being treated unfairly.
* The result? Barr jumped into action, working to secure a lighter sentence for a key Trump ally.

**This is absolutely unacceptable. We cannot have the rule of law change at the whim of Trump's Twitter rants.** And that comes after he misled the American people about what was in the Mueller report. He has violated the promise he made during his confirmation hearing to _"keep the enforcement process sacrosanct from political influence."_ Because he clearly is willing to sacrifice the independence of our professional prosecutors in the service of Donald Trump and his allies, he is unfit to serve as Attorney General. **[Sign the petition and let's make sure we're heard: William Barr must resign _now_. ](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f792/6cd27893/53e0b92c/15866980/1288247546/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA | Have you chipped in yet to support Doug Jones? Team, just making sure you saw Cory's note from a few days ago. Since he sent this email, we've seen an overwhelming show of support for Doug Jones -- who can really use our help right now after his courageous vote to convict Donald Trump. **[Let's see if we can keep it up. Send in a split donation between Cory's campaign and Doug Jones today.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/corybooker-2020-dougjones-split?refcode=em_fr_20200216_BHQ_20200216-fr-BHQ-EM1-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **There 's absolutely no way we can let Jeff Sessions or Roy Moore claim this Senate seat in November.** Thanks for helping to make sure Doug has the resources to compete -- and win -- this critical election in Alabama. Booker HQ _\---FORWARDED MESSAGE---_ Team, Yesterday, Senator Doug Jones made the courageous decision to vote to remove Donald Trump from office for his abuse of power. Doug holds a very competitive Senate seat in Alabama, one of the reddest states in the country. He is Alabama's first Democratic senator in nearly two decades and he's up for re-election this fall. It would've been politically expedient for him to vote against convicting the President, who is very popular in Alabama. But Doug did the right thing despite what it might mean for his re-election chances. **Now it 's on us to help Doug defend his Senate seat against Jeff Sessions and Roy Moore, both of whom are running against him. Split a donation between both of our campaigns right now.** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | [](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/corybooker-2020-dougjones-split?refcode=em_fr_20200216_BHQ_20200216-fr-BHQ-EM1-actives_actives_express5&amount=5&express_lane=true&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=&amounts=5,10,22,50,100")

I've seen firsthand through our work together in the Senate that Doug is a dedicated public servant who represents his state honorably. I actually had the privilege of campaigning with him in Alabama back in 2017 when he ran for his seat and saw for myself how dedicated he is to his constituents.
In these uncertain times, Doug upheld his oath to protect and defend the Constitution -- showing us what true leadership really looks like.
Unfortunately, that isn't going to stop Jeff Sessions, Roy Moore, and the other Republicans currently in this race. They're already attacking Doug for his gutsy vote yesterday. **This will be a very difficult election -- and Doug needs our help.**
**[There's no doubt that Donald Trump and his allies are going to do everything they can to try and win back this Senate seat. Chip in now to help Doug have the resources to fight back.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/corybooker-2020-dougjones-split?refcode=em_fr_20200216_BHQ_20200216-fr-BHQ-EM1-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
I hope to have the honor of serving with Doug in the Senate for another term.
Cory

name: Karen Martinez, NV for Tom
subject: Your Sunday update
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
---
Hi all — I’m Karen, the Nevada digital director for Tom 2020. Yesterday marked the start of early voting in Nevada (and one week until the caucuses!), and the energy on the ground is amazing. I am proud to see Tom standing for what is morally right and helping the immigrant community. He is a kind and caring individual who does the right thing even when it’s difficult — and that’s exactly what we need from our next president. Here are your campaign updates for today! 📩 One of the things I think is so wonderful about Tom is that he _really listens_. He spends most of his time talking with people like you and me, asking about the challenges we face, the changes we want to see, what we’re concerned about, what we’re excited about. So today, I’m writing Tom a quick thank you for taking the time to hear us out. Want to join me? subject: Why I'm endorsing Jessica Cisneros
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
My grandmother came to San Antonio as a seven-year-old orphan from Mexico almost 100 years ago. I was born and raised here. It's where I served on the city council and as Mayor and it's where I'm prou | | |
---
| |
---
| | Friend, My grandmother came to San Antonio as a seven-year-old orphan from Mexico almost 100 years ago. I was born and raised here. It's where I served on the city council and as Mayor and it's where I'm proud to call home. I know this community, and I know that it deserves leaders who will put the people who live here first, above all else. Jessica is a fearless and progressive leader who will fight for the people of South Texas. She'll stand up for immigrants, for hardworking families, and for those who need a voice in Washington. That's why I'm proud to endorse Jessica Cisneros to represent South Texas in Congress and I'm asking you to join me in supporting her campaign. [**Can you make a donation to her campaign today? The election is just a couple weeks away, and more than ever, Jessica needs our grassroots support >>**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc92b/3eb3e2fc/6a3344dc/744ea825/2963129010/VEsH/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-castro-jessica-cisneros?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=1+-+Can+you+make+a+donation+to+her+campaign+&utm_campaign=20200216EM&source=20200216EM") She will help us build an America where everyone is seen and everyone counts. She'll push back on Donald Trump's cruel and inhumane immigration policies. She will work tirelessly every single day so that South Texas families can get better health care, good paying jobs, and an affordable education. Washington only works if the people that we elect stand up for the communities that sent them there. Jessica will do just that. Jessica embodies the American Dream. She's the daughter of Mexican immigrants who became a human rights lawyer. She understands the challenges the people of Texas and this country are facing because she's lived them herself. I know she will make us proud in Congress. So I'm asking you to help her get across the finish line before her primary on March 3: [**Jessica 's campaign is powered by people like you. Pitch in $5, $10, or $20 to help get her across the finish line. Every cent of your contribution will go directly to her campaign.**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc92b/3eb3e2fc/6a3344dc/744ea824/2963129010/VEsE/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-castro-jessica-cisneros?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2+-+Jessicarsquos+campaign+is+powered+by+peo&utm_campaign=20200216EM&source=20200216EM") Thank you for joining Jessica in this fight. I cannot wait to call her our congresswoman. Together, we will win! For the Future, Julian Castro

| Dear Patriot,
As I am sure you already know, the Republican primary for Pennsylvania's 7th Congressional District is heating up.
Here is who I am up against:
* Lisa Scheller, a "Never-Trumper" and pro-abortion RINO, who was afraid even to mention the President in her announcement speech.

* Matt Connolly, who has raised less than $5,000 to run (against Susan Wild's millions) and is carpetbagging into the PA-7th after losing twice in another district.

I embrace a competitive primary, but neither of these two can defeat liberal Susan Wild.
Fellow Patriot, I need your support today to ensure that the best candidate to defeat Rep. Susan Wild is elected in the primary.
Will you help me win this fight by donating $50, $25, or even just $10 to my campaign?
---

How is he going to be able to stand up against a well-funded incumbent like Rep. Susan Wild? Lisa Scheller, on the other hand, is the epitome of the Establishment and is a Never-Trumper who donated the maximum to John Kasich in the primary against President Trump.
Scheller believes her path to victory includes running away from President Trump.
We aren't going to defeat socialist Susan Wild by running a liberal Republican like Scheller. My strategy, on the other hand, is to support Conservative values even in the face of opposition and to be rock solid in my support for our President Donald J. Trump.
The Lehigh Valley needs a candidate who will clearly state what they believe and what they want to accomplish and who will fight tirelessly for those goals and not waver under political pressure.
I am running for Congress in the Pennsylvania 7th Congressional District to be an ally of President Trump as he fights to Make and Keep America Great. But I cannot do it without your support. I urge you, after hearing more about my fellow Republican challengers, to chip into my campaign.
A donation will go a long way in showing I am the right candidate for the job, and that I am the only candidate who can defeat Rep. Susan Wild in 2020.
Please consider donating $50, $25, or even just $10 today to help my campaign.

For Freedom,

body:
When you make a contribution to our campaign online, you are helping to keep my “call time” to a minimum. That’s why these emails are so important.
#
When you start a campaign for U.S. Senate, you get a lot of advice. One of the things you’ll hear from all the experts is, _“You’ve got to spend half your day on call time.”_ Dialing for dollars is how these big campaigns get funded.
What they don’t talk about is what you have to give up to do that “call time.” Every minute I’m on the phone is a minute I could be spending in Paducah or Paintsville; Bardstown or Berea. That’s valuable time with the voters who could flip Kentucky and defeat Mitch McConnell — if we reach them.
**When you make a contribution to our campaign online, you are helping to keep my “call time” to a minimum.** That’s why these emails are so important. If we raise enough online, we can spend our time talking to voters, talking to the media, and spreading the word on social media.
I’ve got a big fundraising goal to reach this month, and I’m wondering now if I can get your help over email so I can spend more of my time on the campaign trail:
[**Can you make a $5 donation right now to help us defeat Mitch McConnell? clean_body: When you make a contribution to our campaign online, you are helping to keep my “call time” to a minimum. That’s why these emails are so important. When you start a campaign for U.S. Senate, you get a lot of advice. One of the things you’ll hear from all the experts is, “You’ve got to spend half your day on call time.” Dialing for dollars is how these big campaigns get funded. What they don’t talk about is what you have to give up to do that “call time.” Every minute I’m on the phone is a minute I could be spending in Paducah or Paintsville; Bardstown or Berea. That’s valuable time with the voters who could flip Kentucky and defeat Mitch McConnell — if we reach them. When you make a contribution to our campaign online, you are helping to keep my “call time” to a minimum. That’s why these emails are so important. If we raise enough online, we can spend our time talking to voters, talking to the media, and spreading the word on social media. I’ve got a big fundraising goal to reach this month, and I’m wondering now if I can get your help over email so I can spend more of my time on the campaign trail: = = Look, a lot of people open these emails and choose not to give. That’s fine. We know some of you can’t give each time we ask. But I can’t stress enough how important these fundraising emails are if we are going to win in November. Online contributions help us run the kind of visible, engaged, and inspiring campaign we need to defeat Mitch McConnell. = Thank you for chipping in today. It’s a huge stress reliever to be able to spend less time on the phone and more time talking to voters. \- Charles Booker = | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Booker for Kentucky

>From Your Capitol - February 17, 2020

The sixth week of the 2020 legislative session brings us to the first major legislative deadline, the last day for legislators to request bills written by legislative services. The next major deadline is Monday, February 17, which is the last day a bill may be submitted for consideration for this session.

The process for a bill to be considered by the legislature begins with an idea. Sometimes these ideas come from state or national groups, but often the ideas come from citizens who have contacted a legislator.

The legislator takes that idea and begins a discussion with the House Legislative Services which consist of attorneys who work for the Mississippi House of Representatives. Their job is to research the legal aspects of the idea and provide the legislator with a report of their findings. It then becomes the responsibility of the legislator to determine if he wants to have the attorney write the bill.

Once the bill is written the legislator begins discussing his bill with other legislators and gaining support. Some ideas become very popular and other legislators will “sign on” to the bill. Once others have “signed on” they become co-authors or sometime referred to as co-sponsors of the bill.

Before the deadline, the original author will “drop” the bill. This phrase comes from the actual process of putting a paper copy of the bill in a box located on the floor of the House chamber. The bill is actually dropped into the box. At this point the bill becomes the property of the House and will begin the process of being assigned to a committee for review and hopefully voted on by the entire body of the House of Representatives.

What ideas do you have for legislation that would help our state?

Bill of the Week

Mississippi Seafood Marketing Law of 2020, House Bill 551

clean_body: To read this newsletter in HTML format, click new laws do you think the Mississippi legislature should consider? From Your Capitol - February 17, 2020 The sixth week of the 2020 legislative session brings us to the first major legislative deadline, the last day for legislators to request bills written by legislative services. The next major deadline is Monday, February 17, which is the last day a bill may be submitted for consideration for this session. The process for a bill to be considered by the legislature begins with an idea. Sometimes these ideas come from state or national groups, but often the ideas come from citizens who have contacted a legislator. The legislator takes that idea and begins a discussion with the House Legislative Services which consist of attorneys who work for the Mississippi House of Representatives. Their job is to research the legal aspects of the idea and provide the legislator with a report of their findings. It then becomes the responsibility of the legislator to determine if he wants to have the attorney write the bill. Once the bill is written the legislator begins discussing his bill with other legislators and gaining support. Some ideas become very popular and other legislators will “sign on” to the bill. Once others have “signed on” they become co-authors or sometime referred to as co-sponsors of the bill. Before the deadline, the original author will “drop” the bill. This phrase comes from the actual process of putting a paper copy of the bill in a box located on the floor of the House chamber. The bill is actually dropped into the box. At this point the bill becomes the property of the House and will begin the process of being assigned to a committee for review and hopefully voted on by the entire body of the House of Representatives. What ideas do you have for legislation that would help our state? Bill of the Week Mississippi Seafood Marketing Law of 2020, House Bill 551 Friends for Dana Criswell PO Box 1321 Olive Branch, MS 38654 Dana@DanaCriswell.net

name: Jason Yates
subject: George Washington’s charge to a nation
body:
| | Rest on the pillars of religion and morality
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvNpONPvZrBWWxUdHST8-zHHGDZBczNn_37GXHs6h31aDQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200217com01&n=1)
| Peter, Presidents Day was originally created in 1885 to celebrate George Washington’s Birthday. Today it is viewed as a day to honor all American Presidents. It is an opportunity for us to remember those who have served our nation, reflect on their success and failures, and it is a chance for us to thank God for a system of government that has allowed us to have peaceful transfers of power. In September 1796, worn out by burdens of the presidency and attacks of political foes, George Washington announced his decision not to seek a third term. With the assistance of Alexander Hamilton and James Madison, Washington composed in a “Farewell Address” his political charge to the nation. Washington’s address included his carefully thought out counsel to the American people as they would ensure the success of the great American experiment. He reminded them that religion and morality must remain the foundation and the fabric of our society. [Washington’s words](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvNpONPvZrBWWxUdHST8-zHHGDZBczNn_37GXHs6h31aDQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200217com01&n=2), spoken over 200 years ago, are just as relevant to Americans today. In fact, since 1896, Washington’s Farewell Address has been read every year on the floor of the Senate by a United States Senator. This takes place close to Presidents Day each year on February 22, commemorating Washington’s birthday. The member selected to read the address alternates between political parties. I hope you will take just a minute today to learn more about Washington’s decision and to read an excerpt from Washington’s poignant Farewell Address. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvNpONPvZrBWWxUdHST8-zHHGDZBczNn_37GXHs6h31aDQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200217com01&n=3) Thank you for joining with us, millions of Christians united across the nation, to bring our faith forward into the public square. May we heed the words of our nation’s first President. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200217com01&n=4)

name: Wesley Hunt
subject: Honoring Our Presidents
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

Today, as we enjoy spending time with friends and family, we take the time to reflect on our past leaders. From Washington to Lincoln to Reagan, our Nation has been blessed with strong leaders to fight for our Democracy.

But Presidents' Day holds special meaning for me this year. Just last week, President Trump endorsed my campaign to replace Lizzie Fletcher.

Friend, we need your help to get to Washington to support the President’s agenda. The Democratic party has moved radically to the left. Instead of working with President Trump to benefit cities like Houston, Democrats are pursuing an agenda that includes government run healthcare, open borders and a Green New Deal that will kill Houston’s energy jobs.

President Trump knows that we need leaders in Washington who can actually get things done, not politicians like Lizzie Fletcher.

With early voting [beginning tomorrow,](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=b793303515d44ddf9d6e673e12d1dd8b&_e=N2Y8dntd-UxEWWVanfwZBsF-R2JghjIfyPjzwYuVTKmOeREGIPXY9OR6wTq7QLK7G8tlNBYV4_ce1gcGOOfz8Nvv76S0uAsqB9h5H2lmykIpmqCOmS5EjWGhODXd8IAaWLujOLl15_OAXdXBAFxO3IJgaEfmB2z62NTd9Kjca2YLjP2_NPIw6OkiGO53L_i9PfB2Zdgm8VHxD1a_vLbtatAfaMLwgM89XZgGIrHgwsFH0z-3pJ9LNQO-Kfb9-cByDlTYx-CiqS2v4jLeetBNrA%3D%3D) I humbly ask for your vote.

Sincerely,
Wesley Hunt

name: Filemon Vela
subject: Bold leadership for South Texas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Filemon Vela for Congress
body:
| | | Friend,
Election season is in full swing and I'm asking for your support to continue fighting for South Texas. It's an honor to represent this district and I know bold leadership is the only way we can continue to make progress on critical issues facing our communities.
Whether it's lowering the cost of prescription drugs for our seniors, strengthening economic opportunities for both rural and urban areas, or highlighting the growing crisis on our border, I'm ready to roll up my sleeves and continue these important fights.
As a member of the House Armed Services Committee, I'm also committed to standing with all of those who serve or who have served. In addition to supporting the missions of our bases here in South Texas, we're also traveling across the district to deliver overdue recognition to our Vietnam War veterans for their brave service to our nation.
I can't do it alone though. I need your help to highlight the work we're doing in every corner of the district. [If you haven't already, help us prepare for the fights ahead by following our campaign Facebook page.](https://act.myngp.com/el/5buLo2PWQ_9U1eHxPUDOHnHSRIa-cwVuV4vTEhIPR1E=/_WX6MxWVgCWfeeiKTixgSuJnakbw95pPZbXhqutVMh4=)
Together, I can't wait to see what we accomplish in 2020!
Fil

| | Hey Peter,

I'm on the road today, but I wanted to send you a quick email because early voting in the Texas primary starts TOMORROW and we're coming up on an urgent fundraising deadline.

**We only have until midnight to raise the $13,192 remaining toward our pre-primary goal.[Can you donate right now to help us get there?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905b9/7f51f7bc/4dc82b5a/4ef1b8f9/2555791497/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkyOSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-mm-2020?refcode=email_20200217.1nd__929__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Let me tell you quickly why it's so important. Texas is the biggest battleground state, and according to _The Washington Post_ this Senate seat is one of the top ten most likely to flip.

But it's not just going to happen -- we have to build an incredibly strong campaign to beat a three-term incumbent. That means raising enough money _right now_ to reach undecided voters across Texas for the primary, and then hit the ground running in the next phase of the campaign.

**[I know I can count on y'all, so that's why I'm emailing to ask you to dig deep and give if you can before tonight's midnight deadline.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648905b9/7f51f7bc/4dc82b5a/4ef1b8f9/2555791497/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjkyOSJ9/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-feb-mm-2020?refcode=email_20200217.1nd__929__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Your support means the world.

\-- MJ

clean_body: Here at the Holcomb Crew, President’s Day is a big deal. We love honoring the leaders who have led our republic through thick and thin. Friend, Happy President’s Day! Here at the Holcomb Crew, President’s Day is a big deal. We love honoring the leaders who have led our republic through thick and thin. Eric is the biggest presidential history buff we know (he had his bachelor party in Springfield, Illinois — Abraham Lincoln’s home for 24 years...after he lived in Indiana). This year, he was finally able to complete a collection of signatures from every single person to hold the office. But knowing him, he won’t be able to stop himself from starting another collection forever. We want to hear what Hoosiers have to say on the subject. Friend, what presidential item should Eric collect next? If you submit an answer, we’ll follow up with you at the end of the week and let you know the winner and what Eric thinks of your suggestion! I rarely mince my words when it comes to Donald Trump, friend, so let me say this: every day that he has been in office, Mr. Trump has further destroyed the integrity of the Office of the Presidency. He has corrupted our democracy and our values. He’s making a mockery of what it means to be an American. Today serves as a stark reminder of just how much work we have to do to restore our founding government to one that’s truly “of, by, and for the people.” But our job — our singular task, the most urgent thing on all of our plates — is to end Mr. Trump’s presidency right here, this November. With our votes, we can bring about real change in this country, but it will take a concerted effort. I’m ready to do everything in my power to defeat Mr. Trump and get our country back on track, and I hope you’ll stick with me as we do this work together. He has corrupted our democracy and our values. He’s making a mockery of what it means to be an American. Today serves as a stark reminder of just how much work we have to do to restore our founding government to one that’s truly “of, by, and for the people.” But our job — our singular task, the most urgent thing on all of our plates — is to end Mr. Trump’s presidency right here, this November. body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kjVc16AJ8Uw&t=29s&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927
Field Director Ramiro Ojeda discusses his role in Marc Flores' campaign.

Good afternoon Peter,
As you know, early voting for the March primary begins tomorrow. As Marc's field director, I want to thank everyone who has stepped up and volunteered or contributed over the last several months.

We have knocked on thousands of doors, and raised just over $100,000 thus far. With the primary drawing near, I need your help to pave a clear path to victory.

The only way for us to win is to continue to be present throughout the district, both in person and online. Marc and I, as well as our outstanding volunteers, are out canvassing voters every day. We need your help to reach those we won't have time to talk to in person.

If each of our supporters donates $50 today, we can fund our advertisements and mailers through the end of the campaign, and have a clear shot at winning. (http://actblue.com/donate/marcflores?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927)

**

Express Donate: $25 (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927)

Express Donate: $50 (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927)

Express Donate: $100 (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927)

Or, donate another amount (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/marcflores?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other&utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927)

------------------------------------------------------------
VOLUNTEER
If you have already contributed, or feel that you can help out in another way, please volunteer (https://www.marcfloresforcongress.com/volunteer/?utm_source=Marc+Flores+for+Congress&utm_campaign=4e85fb31f5-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2020_02_17_02_39&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_b08c1b6182-4e85fb31f5-32267927) to help us reach out to voters. The more people we have to help out, the more people we'll reach.
I can't thank you enough for all of your help in getting us to where we are today. In the time I've come to know Marc, I have seen not only a leader, but an advocate. He is exactly who we need representing our district.

See you at the polls!

Ramiro Ojeda
Field Director
Marc Flores for Congress

subject: Happy President's Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DAVID HILL FOR OKLAHOMA, INC | | [](http://links
body:
I want to reach out to personally wish you a happy President's Day. Today we can look back at all of the significant accomplishments President Trump has made for our country, along with the presidents who came before him. The democracy that George Washington built in America is what makes our nation great. His vision for America, to be free and prosperous, helped to lay the foundation for our strong institutions. Abraham Lincoln fought hard for the rights of all Americans and has inspired our country to continually fight for equality. A healthy democracy and equal rights are beliefs that all Americans have in common. Our strength to uphold these virtues, along with our freedom, is what makes this country so prosperous today. May we all have a safe and happy President’s Day filled with pride for this incredible nation. Sincerely,
David Hill PAID FOR BY DAVID HILL FOR OKLAHOMA, INC | |
